query: Cognizant job posting (artificial intelligence OR machine learning OR generative AI OR LLM OR data scientist)

results.title: Cognizant AI Factory Co-Op – Career Center | Mississippi State University
highlights:
Cognizant AI Factory Co-Op – Career Center | Mississippi State University
[...]
# Cognizant AI Factory Co-Op
[...]
The Cognizant AI Factory is an innovation-led unit that rapidly designs and delivers AI-powered solutions for enterprise clients. We are looking for a technically capable AI/ML associate to develop quick-turnaround Proof-of-Concept (POC) solutions that support client engagements – independently of delivery teams, using our dedicated AI environment.
[...]
You will take use cases emerging from client discussions and translate them into functional, demonstrable prototypes using synthetic data and modern GenAI tooling.
[...]
This role offers hands-on exposure to live enterprise client engagements across industries including Insurance, Banking, Healthcare, and Retail. Interns will have access to a dedicated AI Factory environment equipped with enterprise-grade tools and models, along with structured mentorship from experienced AI architects and solution leads. High-performing candidates may also be considered for conversion to a full-time role based on performance and business need.
[...]
· Build AI/ML proof-of-concept applications rapidly based on client-identified use cases
[...]
· Develop agentic AI workflows (single and multi-agent) using frameworks such as LangChain, LangGraph, AutoGen, or CrewAI
[...]
· Integrate LLMs via APIs (OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, Anthropic) to power intelligent, task-driven solutions
[...]
· Implement RAG pipelines using vector databases (FAISS, ChromaDB, Pinecone) where applicable
[...]
· Work with synthetic datasets to simulate real-world business scenarios
[...]
· Present POC outputs through lightweight demo interfaces or structured notebooks
[...]
· Strong Python programming skills
[...]
· Hands-on knowledge of LLMs, prompt engineering, and API integration
[...]
· Experience with at least one agentic AI framework (LangChain, AutoGen, CrewAI, etc.)
[...]
· Familiarity with RAG architectures and vector databases
[...]
· Exposure to cloud platforms (Azure, AWS, or GCP) is a plus
[...]
· Applicants must be pursuing a bachelor’s or master’s degree with an expected graduation date of May 2026 or December 2026 or have obtained a degree within the previous 12 months.
[...]
· B.E. / B.Tech / M.Tech / MCA in Computer Science, AI, Data Science, or related field
[...]
· A strong GitHub portfolio or project work in applied AI/ML will be given significant weightage
[...]
The co-op will last 12 weeks, starting in June 2026.

results.title: Consulting Manager – AI Engineer – Insurance & Retirement – Office of Career Strategy – Yale University
highlights:
# Consulting Manager – AI Engineer – Insurance & Retirement – Office of Career Strategy – Yale University
[...]
Consulting Manager – AI Engineer – Insurance & Retirement – Office of Career Strategy – Yale University
[...]
Cognizant
[...]
# Consulting Manager – AI Engineer – Insurance & Retirement
[...]
About Cognizant Consulting
[...]
Cognizant Consulting is more than Cognizant’s consulting practice-we’re a global community of 5,000+ experts dedicated to helping clients reimagine their business. Blending our deep industry and technology advisory capability, we create innovative business solutions for Fortune 500 clients. And now, we’re looking for our next colleague who’ll join us in shaping the future of business. Could it be you?
[...]
As an Consulting Manager – Artificial Intelligence (AI) Technical Architect – Insurance & Retirement , you will make an impact by designing and guiding the delivery of secure, scalable AI-powered applications that solve complex business problems for insurance and retirement clients . You will be a valued member of the Insurance & Retirement Consulting team and work collaboratively with product leaders, data and engineering teams, enterprise architects, and client stakeholders to move AI solutions from concept to production.
[...]
- Lead end-to-end AI solution architecture for custom applications-creating solution blueprints, reference architectures, and detailed designs that connect models, data pipelines, application layers, and integrations.
[...]
- Define AI platform and model strategy by selecting appropriate approaches (machine learning, generative AI, large language models), orchestration patterns, inference strategies, and lifecycle management that meet enterprise standards.
[...]
- Architect data and integration patterns for AI use cases, including ingestion, feature engineering, training pipelines, and real-time/batch inference-integrated with core systems through APIs and event-driven approaches.
[...]
What you must have to be considered
[...]
- 7-10 years in software engineering and/or architecture, with strong experience designing cloud-native applications (AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud Platform), microservices, APIs, and modern DevOps practices.
[...]
- Hands-on experience designing and implementing AI solutions , including machine learning and generative AI/large language model solutions, with practical model orchestration patterns.
[...]
- Experience designing machine learning operations (MLOps) approaches for model training, evaluation, deployment, monitoring, and continuous improvement in production environments.
[...]
- Strong data architecture knowledge (data lakes/warehouses, streaming, feature stores, vector databases) and ability to design robust data flows for AI use cases.
[...]
- Experience delivering solutions in life insurance and/or retirement (industry value chain, data considerations, and regulatory constraints).
[...]
- Exposure to emerging enterprise AI patterns such as agentic AI , AI copilots , and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) .
- Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Data Science, or a related field.
[...]
- Strong familiarity with enterprise security, privacy, and governance expectations for AI solutions.
[...]
- Track record of building reusable accelerators, best practices, and reference architectures that improve delivery speed and quality.

results.title: Senior Agentic AI Developer | Hirevector
highlights:
Senior Agentic AI Developer | Hirevector
[...]
# Senior Agentic AI Developer
[...]
At Cognizant, our global community sets us apart—an energetic, collaborative and inclusive workplace where everyone can thrive
[...]
Cognizant is right where you
[...]
and skills and are looking for an opportunity
[...]
Cognizant Technology Solutions is looking for a AGENTIC AI DEVELOPER to join our dynamic team. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to drive innovation and deliver impactful solutions that align with our company’s goals.
[...]
AGENTIC AI DEVELOPER
[...]
In this role, you will be responsible for launching and implementing GenAI agentic solutions aimed at reducing the risk and cost of managing large-scale production environments with varying complexities. You will address various production runtime challenges by developing agentic AI solutions that can diagnose, reason, and take actions in production environments to improve productivity and address issues related to production support.
[...]
· Build agentic AI systems: Design and implement tool-calling agents that combine retrieval, structured reasoning, and secure action execution (function calling, change orchestration, policy enforcement) following MCP protocol. Engineer robust guardrails for safety, compliance, and least-privilege access.
[...]
· Productionize LLMs: Build evaluation framework for open-source and foundational LLMs; implement retrieval pipelines, prompt synthesis, response validation, and self-correction loops tailored to production operations.
[...]
· Integrate with runtime ecosystems: Connect agents to observability, incident management, and deployment systems to enable automated diagnostics, runbook execution, remediation, and post-incident summarization with full traceability.
[...]
· Collaborate directly with users: Partner with production engineers, and application teams to translate production pain points into agentic AI roadmaps; define objective functions linked to reliability, risk reduction, and cost; and deliver auditable, business-aligned outcomes.
[...]
· Safety, reliability, and governance: Build validator models, adversarial prompts, and policy checks into the stack; enforce deterministic fallbacks, circuit breakers, and rollback strategies; instrument continuous evaluations for usefulness, correctness, and risk.
[...]
· Scale and performance: Optimize cost and latency via prompt engineering, context management, caching, model routing, and distillation; leverage batching, streaming, and parallel tool-calls to meet stringent SLOs under real-world load.
[...]
· Build a RAG pipeline: Curate domain-knowledge; build data-quality validation framework; establish feedback loops and milestone framework maintain knowledge freshness.
[...]
· 5+ years of software development in one or more languages (Python, C/C++, Go, Java); strong hands-on experience building and maintaining large-scale Python applications preferred.
[...]
· 3+ years designing, architecting, testing, and launching production ML systems, including model deployment/serving, evaluation and monitoring, data processing pipelines, and model fine-tuning workflows.
[...]
· Practical experience with Large Language Models (LLMs): API integration, prompt engineering, fine-tuning/adaptation, and building applications using RAG and tool-using agents (vector retrieval, function calling, secure tool execution).
[...]
· Understanding of different LLMs, both commercial and open source, and their capabilities (e.g., OpenAI, Gemini, Llama, Qwen, Claude).
[...]
· Solid grasp of applied statistics, core ML concepts, algorithms, and data structures to deliver efficient and reliable solutions.
[...]
· Preferred: Proficiency building and operating on cloud infrastructure (ideally AWS), including containerized services (ECS/EKS), serverless (Lambda), data services (S3, DynamoDB, Redshift), orchestration (Step Functions), model serving (SageMaker), and infra-as-code (Terraform/CloudFormation).

results.title: Quality Engineer AI – Career Advising & Professional Development | MIT
highlights:
Cognizant
[...]
# Quality Engineer AI
[...]
Quality Engineer (AI & Test Automation)
[...]
About Cognizant’s Quality Engineering & Assurance Team
[...]
Our Quality Engineering & Assurance team is the innovative engine at our company. We are a dedicated group of builders and problem-solvers responsible for the most critical, customer-facing platforms. Within this team, the Quality practice is not a downstream function but a fully integrated partner in development. Our mission is to embed quality into every stage of the lifecycle, and as a Quality Engineer, you will be on the front lines of this effort.
[...]
Are you a driven Quality Engineer passionate about building the future of testing? We are seeking a creative and hands-on QE to be a key contributor to our quality practice. This is an exciting opportunity to move beyond traditional QA and dive into the world of AI-driven testing, helping us build and maintain robust quality standards for our mission-critical intelligent applications that are redefining customer interaction.
[...]
- Implement and execute test plans for AI/ML applications: Support QA activities and certify the quality of AI-powered systems, including hands-on testing of chatbots for intent recognition, conversational flow, response accuracy, and edge case handling.
[...]
- Leverage modern AI tools to enhance workflow: Utilize AI code assistants like GitHub Copilot to accelerate test script development and explore generative AI for tasks such as test data creation and bug report summarization.
[...]
- Ensure AI reliability and fairness: Execute test cases to identify issues related to bias, fairness, and model robustness, contributing to the overall trustworthiness of AI systems.
[...]
Required Skills & Qualifications
[...]
- Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related technical field, or equivalent practical experience.
[...]
- A keen interest in or prior experience testing AI-powered applications, such as chatbots or other AI/ML-based systems.
[...]
- A passion for learning and staying up to date with the latest trends in AI and software testing.
[...]
Cognizant is always looking for top talent. We are searching for candidates to fill future needs within the business. This job posting represents potential future employment opportunities with Cognizant. Although the position is not currently available, we want to provide you with the opportunity to express your interest in future employment opportunities with Cognizant. If a job opportunity that you may be qualified for becomes available in the future, we will notify you. At that time you can determine whether you would like to apply for the specific open position. Thank you for your interest in Cognizant career opportunities.

results.title: Cognizant Launches Ace Team Program to Develop Cohort of AI Builders - Jun 3, 2026
highlights:
Cognizant Launches Ace Team Program to Develop Cohort of AI Builders
[...]
High-impact career track for future AI builders who will drive the next phase of digital transformation
[...]
BENGALURU, India, June 3, 2026 / PRNewswire/ -- Cognizant (NASDAQ: CTSH) today announced the launch of the Cognizant Ace Team Program, a strategic initiative designed to build a cohort of top engineering minds who will deliver cutting-edge digital transformation for clients and play a central role in the company's evolution into an AI builder organization.
[...]
is structured as a combination of
[...]
selective hiring program,
[...]
elite talent pathway and
[...]
The program is designed to build a community of high-potential engineers who will work on real client challenges using AI-native tools, modern architectures and outcome-driven methods. Ace Team members are expected to be deployed in focused, high-impact teams and be looked upon to get critical initiatives off the ground, contributing directly to pioneering and emerging technology solutions that drive meaningful business outcomes for clients.
[...]
With an India-focused launch, the Ace Team will serve as the cornerstone of Cognizant's shift into an AI builder organization, fueling transformation across the company's three-vector strategy of Hyperproductivity, Industrializing AI and Agentification through a high-caliber, AI-ready engineering force. The program is designed to enable the creation of high-value, niche AI offerings and support the execution of complex, large-scale technology initiatives, while accelerating innovation across the ecosystem. Its impact will include accelerating time to market, advancing end-to-end modernization and the development of future-ready platforms that support scale, reliability and sustained innovation.
[...]
Rajesh Varrier, President – Global Operations and Chairman & Managing Director, Cognizant India, said, "The launch of the Cognizant Ace Team program marks a critical step in strengthening our engineering depth as enterprises move rapidly from AI experimentation to real-world execution. Delivering measurable, scalable AI outcomes increasingly depends on AI-ready skills and strong engineering fundamentals. Through Ace Team, we are building a differentiated talent engine that directly supports our three-vector strategy of Hyperproductivity, Industrializing AI and Agentification, with the goal of enabling us to take AI from concepts to production and apply it at scale across industries."
[...]
Selection to the Cognizant Ace Team program is based on advanced technical competency, AI fluency and strong communication skills, rather than institution-based criteria. The multi-stage evaluation process includes communication assessment, aptitude assessment, technical assessment, and technical interviews and is designed to gauge industry-ready capabilities such as full-stack AI development, data structures and algorithms, prompt engineering, retrieval-augmented generation, agentic AI and modern front-end development.
[...]
Selected candidates can be hired into differentiated roles with distinct career paths, deployment models and types of work. Ace Team members are expected to join as full-stack AI Engineers and begin contributing to real client engagements early in their journey, working on production codebases and building AI‑native capabilities including RAG pipelines, agentic workflows and LLM‑integrated applications across industries.
[...]
About Cognizant Cognizant (Nasdaq: CTSH) is an AI Builder and technology services provider, bridging the gap between AI investment and enterprise value by building full-stack AI solutions for our clients. Our deep industry, process and engineering expertise enables us to build an organization's unique context into technology systems that amplify human potential, drive tangible outcomes and keep global enterprises ahead in a fast-changing world. See how at www.cognizant.ai or @cognizant.

results.title: Cognizant Develops Frontier Certified Engineer and Frontier Business Operator Roles to Define the Next Generation of AI-Powered Work - Jun 1, 2026
highlights:
Cognizant Develops Frontier Certified Engineer and Frontier Business Operator Roles to Define the Next Generation of AI-Powered Work
[...]
New roles being developed at scale and backed by proprietary SkillSpring training platform, advance Cognizant's AI Builder strategy to close the $4.5 trillion gap between AI capability and enterprise results
[...]
TEANECK, N.J., June 1, 2026 / PRNewswire/ -- Cognizant (NASDAQ: CTSH) a leading AI builder and technology services provider, today announced the creation of two new job categories; Frontier Certified Engineer and Frontier Business Operator, purpose-built for the AI era. The roles represent a significant expansion of Cognizant's workforce strategy and are designed for a new generation of talent at the forefront of enterprise AI transformation.
[...]
Frontier Engineers will work directly with client organizations to reimagine and realign business processes for an AI-enabled environment. Frontier Certified Engineers will specialize in rethinking how work gets done — identifying where AI can reshape operations, eliminate friction, and create lasting competitive advantage. The role requires both strategic thinking and technical fluency, making it one of the most consequential pivot points into the AI-era workforce.
[...]
Frontier Business Operators take an equally critical approach. Tasked with taking full ownership of operational results, they will manage a blended workforce of human and digital labor including deploying AI agents, automation, and human judgment in concert to drive business outcomes. Where Frontier Certified Engineers redesign the map, Frontier Business Operators navigate it in real time.
[...]
irumala Arohi, Chief Learning Officer
[...]

[...]

[...]
workforce architecture. The pyramid was built for a pre-AI world. Frontier Certified Engineers who design for agentic outcomes and the Frontier Business Operators who own them are a new kind of professional, trained from day one to turn AI capability into business reality. The most important innovation of this decade will not come from AI. It will come from empowering
[...]
worker to use it
[...]
Central to the initiative is SkillSpring, Cognizant's proprietary training platform built to onboard and develop talent for emerging AI-era roles. The Frontier Certified Engineer and Frontier Business Operator tracks, which are being built at scale, will undergo structured learning paths combining AI fluency, process design, data interpretation, and operational leadership — equipping them to use and orchestrate AI tools in high-stakes enterprise environments.
[...]
"We are making a deliberate bet on the next generation," said Kathy Diaz, Chief Human Resources Officer of Cognizant. "Frontier Certified Engineers and Frontier Business Operators are roles that are being designed from scratch and at scale for the world we are in now. SkillSpring gives us the ability to rapidly develop associates who can lead in an AI-first environment. This is how we build a workforce that isn't just ready for what's next but is actively shaping it."
[...]
The development of these roles underscores Cognizant's position as an AI builder, a company that builds the human and operational infrastructure enterprises need to make AI work at scale. As organizations across every sector grapple with the challenge of turning AI investments into bottom-line results, Cognizant is investing in the talent model that makes that transition possible.

results.title: Cognizant launches Ace Team Program to nurture new generation of AI talent - The Economic Times
highlights:
Cognizant launches Ace Team Program to nurture new generation of AI talent - The Economic Times
[...]
Business News› Careers› Freshers›Cognizant launches Ace Team Program to nurture new generation of AI talent
[...]
# Cognizant launches Ace Team Program to nurture new generation of AI talent
[...]
Cognizant launches Ace Team Program to nurture new generation of AI talent
[...]
Cognizant has launched the Ace Team Program in India to cultivate engineering talent for client digital transformations and its AI goals. Selected engineers will develop niche AI offerings, support technology initiatives, and drive innovation as full-stack AI Engineers, working on client engagements and building AI-native capabilities.
[...]
Global IT services company Cognizant has launched the Ace Team Program, a talent initiative aimed at identifying and developing engineering talent to support clients' digital transformation efforts and advance the company's AI ambitions. Launched in India, the Ace Team will serve as a key part of Cognizant's transition into an AI builder organization. Under the program, selected engineers will develop niche AI offerings for customers, support large-scale technology initiatives, and help drive innovation across the ecosystem.
[...]
The company said candidate selection will be based on technical capability, AI fluency, and communication effectiveness. A multi-stage assessment process will be used to identify engineers capable of building and deploying next-generation AI solutions.
[...]
In the first phase, the program is open to existing offer holders and select off-campus candidates, including graduates from Tier-1 institutions, with scope for future expansion.Ace Team members will join Cognizant as full-stack AI Engineers and work on client engagements from an early stage. Their responsibilities will include working on production codebases and building AI-native capabilities such as RAG pipelines, agentic workflows, and LLM-integrated applications across industries.

results.title: Lead Data Scientist – Generative AI (GenAI) | Jobspawn
highlights:
Lead Data Scientist – Generative AI (GenAI) | Jobspawn

Jobspawn.← All Jobs

# Lead Data Scientist – Generative AI (GenAI)

Posted Jun 8, 2026

Lead Data Scientist – Generative AI (GenAI)

Experience: 12–15+ Years (Data Science / AI Experience)

Employment Type: Full-Time (W2 Only)

### Location: USA (Hybrid / Onsite)

Work Authorization: H4 EAD, L2S, GC EAD, Green Card, US Citizen

Job Summary

We are seeking a highly accomplished Lead Data Scientist with strong Generative AI expertise to design and drive enterprise-scale AI solutions. The ideal candidate will have deep experience in machine learning, statistical modeling, Large Language Models (LLMs), and advanced analytics, along with strong leadership capabilities to guide teams and deliver impactful AI-driven products.

This role requires ownership of GenAI solution design, model development, experimentation, deployment, and performance optimization across enterprise environments.

- Key Responsibilities
- Define and lead GenAI and data science strategy across business use cases.
- Design and implement LLM-based applications including RAG architectures.
- Lead development of machine learning models and advanced analytics solutions.
- Perform deep exploratory data analysis (EDA) and derive actionable insights.
- Architect end-to-end ML pipelines (data ingestion → training → deployment → monitoring).
- Optimize models for accuracy, scalability, and cost efficiency.
- Collaborate with Data Engineers, ML Engineers, and Product stakeholders.
- Establish best practices for MLOps, model governance, and evaluation frameworks.
- Mentor junior data scientists and lead technical decision-making.
- Drive innovation in AI/ML and Generative AI solutions.

Required Technical Skills

- Data Science & ML
- Strong expertise in Machine Learning & Statistical Modeling
- Python (NumPy, Pandas, Scikit-learn)
- Feature engineering, model evaluation, and tuning

- Generative AI
- LLMs (OpenAI, Anthropic, LLaMA, etc.)
- Prompt engineering and LLM orchestration
- RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation)
- LangChain / LlamaIndex / Hugging Face

- Data & Processing
- SQL
- Large-scale data handling and transformation

- Cloud & MLOps
- AWS / Azure / GCP
- MLflow / SageMaker / Azure ML
- CI/CD for ML pipelines
- Model deployment and monitoring

- Preferred Qualifications
- Experience with Deep Learning (TensorFlow / PyTorch)
- Exposure to vector databases (Pinecone, FAISS, Chroma)
- Experience with Databricks or big data platforms
- Strong leadership, communication, and stakeholder management skills

- Eligibility & Compliance
- W2 Full-Time Only
- No C2C
- No consultancy or vendor profiles

How to Apply

Share your updated resume to:

manish.g@precisiontechcorp.com

Apply for This Job

results.title: Cognizant launches India-focused 'Ace Team' to strengthen AI engineering capabilities
highlights:
Cognizant launches India-focused 'Ace Team' to strengthen AI engineering capabilities

You have successfully subscribed the newsletter.

# Cognizant launches India-focused 'Ace Team' to strengthen AI engineering capabilities

## Cognizant has launched the Ace Team Program in India, a specialized talent initiative aimed at developing AI-ready engineers capable of building and deploying enterprise-scale AI solutions

New Update

Listen to this article

0.75x1x1.5x

00:00/ 00:00

Cognizant has launched the Ace Team Program, a new talent initiative aimed at creating a specialized pool of AI-focused engineers to support enterprise digital transformation projects and strengthen the company's transition into an AI builder organization.

Advertisment

Announced on June 3, 2026, the program combines selective hiring, an elite career pathway, and a centrally governed engineering community aligned with Cognizant's advanced technology strategy. Launched in India, the initiative is designed to identify and develop high-potential engineers capable of building AI-native applications and solving complex client challenges, the company said.

The company said Ace Team members will work on real-world projects using modern software architectures, artificial intelligence tools, and outcome-driven engineering approaches. Participants are expected to contribute to high-impact technology initiatives, including large-scale modernization projects, AI-driven business solutions, and next-generation digital platforms.

According to Cognizant, the program supports its three-pronged strategy focused on Hyperproductivity, Industrializing AI, and Agentification. The company believes the initiative will help accelerate the development of AI-ready talent while enabling faster deployment of enterprise AI solutions.

Advertisment

"The launch of the Cognizant Ace Team program marks a critical step in strengthening our engineering depth as enterprises move rapidly from AI experimentation to real-world execution," said Rajesh Varrier, President – Global Operations and Chairman & Managing Director, Cognizant India. He added that organizations increasingly require AI-ready skills and strong engineering capabilities to deliver scalable AI outcomes.

Selection for the program will be based on technical expertise, AI proficiency, and communication skills rather than educational institution backgrounds. Candidates will undergo a multi-stage evaluation process that includes aptitude tests, communication assessments, technical evaluations, and interviews.

The company said selected candidates will join as full-stack AI engineers and gain early exposure to client projects. Areas of focus will include retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), agentic AI workflows, prompt engineering, large language model (LLM) integration, and modern application development.

Advertisment

In the initial phase, the program will be open to existing offer holders and selected off-campus candidates. Cognizant plans to expand participation over time to include students from premium institutions, partner universities, internal employees, and lateral hires with one to three years of experience.

The initiative comes as technology services firms increasingly compete for AI talent amid growing enterprise demand for production-scale AI deployments and agentic AI applications.

The Ace Team Program reflects Cognizant's broader push to build specialized AI engineering capabilities as enterprises move beyond AI pilots toward large-scale implementation. The initiative also highlights the growing importance of AI-focused workforce development in India's technology services sector.

Advertisment

Role Description As a Product Owner for Sales & Enrollment Platforms, you will make an impact by leading the development and optimization of Elv Sales and Enrollment capabilities. highlights:
AI/ML Engineer /Data Scientist with AI/ML :: Hybrid (Morris Plains NJ, Bloomfield CT, Austin, or Dallas TX office) | Corp to Corp Get all C2C Jobs / hotlists 🔥 Alerts

US jobs, C2C Hotlists, vendor list and Staffing News

Role: AI/ML Engineer /Data Scientist with AI/ML Work Type: Hybrid (Morris Plains NJ, Bloomfield CT, Austin, or Dallas TX office)

Client: Cigna (F2F interview) YOE:10+

Agentic AI experience required. This is most important skill.

Primary Responsibilities:

- Lead the development and training of machine learning models.
- Optimize AI algorithms for performance, efficiency, and scalability.
- Design and implement robust and scalable data pipelines.
- Integrate AI systems with enterprise software and cloud-based applications.
- Drive data-driven decision-making by working with cross-functional teams.
- Plan and lead analytics projects, ensuring alignment with business goals.
- Present findings and insights to stakeholders, translating complex technical results into actionable business recommendations.
- Ensure quality and compliance of data and modeling results with business and regulatory requirements.
- Mentor and guide junior data scientists, fostering technical growth and best practices in AI/ML development.

Required Qualifications & Skills:

- At least 6 years of experience developing AI and ML solutions in a professional setting.
- Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Data Science, or a related field (Master's preferred).
- Experience developing AI-driven chatbots using RAG and AI agents with LangChain.
- Advanced proficiency in Python and SQL for data science applications.
- Strong understanding of machine learning techniques, including supervised, unsupervised, and reinforcement learning.
- Expertise in deep learning architectures (CNNs, RNNs, Transformers) and NLP techniques.
- Proficiency in designing scalable and maintainable AI systems, including microservices and REST API development.
- Hands-on experience with cloud computing platforms such as AWS, Databricks, Azure, and GCP.
- Knowledge of MLOps best practices, including CI/CD pipelines, containerization (Docker, Kubernetes), and role-based access provisioning.
- Strong ability to communicate AI/ML insights and model performance to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

Preferred Qualifications:

- Expertise in mathematical concepts, including statistics, probability, linear algebra, and calculus.
- Experience with distributed computing frameworks such as Apache Spark.
- Familiarity with advanced AI tools and platforms, including PyTorch, Hugging Face, and Kafka.
- Strong project management and leadership skills, with experience driving AI/ML initiatives in a business environment.
- System design expertise, ensuring scalability and robustness in AI deployments.

Thanks & Regards,

Ravikar Yadav

Technical Recruiter

Concept Software and Services Inc.

results.title: Cognizant Launches Elite Ace Team Program in India to Build Full-Stack AI Talent | SightsIn Plus
highlights:
Cognizant Launches Elite Ace Team Program in India to Build Full-Stack AI Talent | SightsIn Plus

2 min. Read

|Jun 6, 2026 11:36 AM

Advertisement

Advertisement

# Cognizant Launches Elite Ace Team Program in India to Build Full-Stack AI Talent

Aa

Global IT services leader Cognizant has launched the “Ace Team” program, a selective elite engineering pathway aimed at cultivating a premium cohort of AI-focused developers.

Advertisement

Advertisement

Rolled out in India, the initiative marks a foundational shift for the company as it pivots from traditional back-office software maintenance into a specialized “AI builder” organization.

## Cognizant Developing Full-Stack AI Engineers

The program combines highly selective recruitment with a centrally governed engineering community designed to build complex, enterprise-ready artificial intelligence solutions.

Candidates selected for the Ace Team will bypass routine IT onboarding to join early-stage client projects as Full-Stack AI Engineers.

Advertisement

Advertisement

Instead of relying on legacy software integration, these engineers will build production-grade, AI-native capabilities.

Their work will span cutting-edge enterprise domains, including Large Language Model (LLM) integrated applications, complex Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) architectures, and autonomous agentic AI workflows capable of planning and reasoning across enterprise operations.

Read also: Deloitte Creates First Chief AI Officer Role Amid Global AI Race

### Skill-First Selection and Premium Salaries

Departing from conventional, university-ranking-based tech hiring, Cognizant is enforcing a strict capability-led selection process.

Advertisement

Advertisement

Candidates undergo a multi-stage evaluation assessing advanced technical proficiency, aptitude, communication skills, and data structures.

Evaluation criteria prioritize specialized competency in prompt engineering and AI orchestration frameworks (such as LangChain) over academic pedigree alone.

To capture elite talent in a fiercely competitive market, Cognizant is introducing aggressive compensation structures.Campus documentation reveals starting salaries ranging from ₹12 Lakhs Per Annum (LPA) for Associate level roles up to ₹18 LPA for Senior Associates.

This represents a massive premium over standard entry-level IT services packages.

### Accelerating Enterprise AI Deployment

The launch addresses a major market evolution, as global clients rapidly transition from exploratory AI pilots to real-world, large-scale execution.

“Delivering measurable, scalable AI outcomes increasingly depends on AI-ready skills and strong engineering fundamentals,” stated Rajesh Varrier, President of Global Operations and Chairman & Managing Director of Cognizant India.

The initial rollout phase targets high-potential 2026 graduates, existing offer holders, and select off-campus candidates from Tier-1 institutions.

Over time, Cognizant plans to scale the Ace Team ecosystem to include premium university partners, lateral industry hires with up to three years of experience, and internal associates.

Advertisement

Advertisement

---

results.title: survival8: Interview at Cognizant for Lead AI Engineer Role (2024 May 22)
highlights:
survival8: Interview at Cognizant for Lead AI Engineer Role (2024 May 22)
[...]
### Generative AI Experience — Semantic Search POC
[...]
- While on the bench at Cognizant (first two months), developed an internal semantic search POC.
- Source corpus: issues and Q&A threads scraped from GitHub and Stack Overflow.
- Questions and answers were converted to vector embeddings and stored in a vector database.
- At query time, the input was embedded and compared against the stored embeddings using similarity search to retrieve closest matches.
- Self-characterised as a "POC-level" GenAI engagement — not a production deployment.
[...]
QCan you walk me through a Generative AI project you've worked on?
[...]
AWhile on bench at Cognizant, I built a semantic search POC. We collected questions and answers from GitHub issues and Stack Overflow, converted them into embeddings, and stored them in a vector database. When a new query came in, we embedded it and retrieved the closest matching entries from the database using similarity search.
[...]
On GenAI experience — semantic search POC
[...]
This is the most damaging part of the interview. You were interviewing for a Lead AI Engineer role in 2024–25 — a role that almost certainly has significant GenAI expectations. You self-described your GenAI background as "not much experience" and spent fewer than five sentences on the only GenAI project you mentioned. You did not name the embedding model used, the vector database, the chunking strategy, the retrieval method (cosine similarity? FAISS? ANN?), or any evaluation approach. You also did not mention any current reading, self-directed learning, or projects in LLMs, RAG pipelines, or LangChain/LangGraph — all of which you've actually explored. This is a credibility-damaging gap for a Lead role.
[...]
The Better Answer
[...]
"The semantic search POC used sentence-transformers — specifically the all-MiniLM-L6-v2 model — to embed GitHub and Stack Overflow Q&A pairs. We stored embeddings in FAISS with an IVF index for efficient approximate nearest-neighbour retrieval. Beyond this POC, I've been deepening my GenAI stack — I've worked with RAG pipeline architectures, explored LangGraph for agentic workflows, and studied LLM evaluation frameworks including RAGAS for retrieval quality measurement. My core ML background in anomaly detection gives me strong fundamentals in embedding spaces and distance-based reasoning, which translates well into modern GenAI retrieval problems."
[...]
The underlying expertise is real — the contamination factor methodology, the ensemble approach, and the PySpark/Hive stack show genuine ML engineering experience. But the presentation of that expertise was significantly below what a Lead AI Engineer role demands. Two changes would have materially improved the outcome: (1) preparing structured, confident narration for each project using a STAR framework, and (2) leading with GenAI competence rather than apologising for its limits. The knowledge is there — the packaging needs work.

results.title: Cognizant Launches Ace Team Program to Develop Cohort of AI Builders
highlights:
BENGALURU, India, June 3, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Cognizant (NASDAQ: CTSH) today announced the launch of the Cognizant Ace Team Program, a strategic initiative designed to build a cohort of top engineering minds who will deliver cutting-edge digital transformation for clients and play a central role in the company's evolution into an AI builder organization.
[...]
The Cognizant Ace Team is structured as a combination of a selective hiring program, an elite talent pathway and a centrally governed engineering community aligned to Cognizant's advanced capability strategy.
[...]
The program is designed to build a community of high-potential engineers who will work on real client challenges using AI-native tools, modern architectures and outcome-driven methods. Ace Team members are expected to be deployed in focused, high-impact teams and be looked upon to get critical initiatives off the ground, contributing directly to pioneering and emerging technology solutions that drive meaningful business outcomes for clients.
[...]
With an India-focused launch, the Ace Team will serve as the cornerstone of Cognizant's shift into an AI builder organization, fueling transformation across the company's three-vector strategy of Hyperproductivity, Industrializing AI and Agentification through a high-caliber, AI-ready engineering force. The program is designed to enable the creation of high-value, niche AI offerings and support the execution of complex, large-scale technology initiatives, while accelerating innovation across the ecosystem. Its impact will include accelerating time to market, advancing end-to-end modernization and the development of future-ready platforms that support scale, reliability and sustained innovation.
[...]
Rajesh Varrier, President – Global Operations and Chairman & Managing Director, Cognizant India, said, "The launch of the Cognizant Ace Team program marks a critical step in strengthening our engineering depth as enterprises move rapidly from AI experimentation to real-world execution. Delivering measurable, scalable AI outcomes increasingly depends on AI-ready skills and strong engineering fundamentals. Through Ace Team, we are building a differentiated talent engine that directly supports our three-vector strategy of Hyperproductivity, Industrializing AI and Agentification, with the goal of enabling us to take AI from concepts to production and apply it at scale across industries."
[...]
Selection to the Cognizant Ace Team program is based on advanced technical competency, AI fluency and strong communication skills, rather than institution-based criteria. The multi-stage evaluation process includes communication assessment, aptitude assessment, technical assessment, and technical interviews and is designed to gauge industry-ready capabilities such as full-stack AI development, data structures and algorithms, prompt engineering, retrieval-augmented generation, agentic AI and modern front-end development.
[...]
Selected candidates can be hired into differentiated roles with distinct career paths, deployment models and types of work. Ace Team members are expected to join as full-stack AI Engineers and begin contributing to real client engagements early in their journey, working on production codebases and building AI‑native capabilities including RAG pipelines, agentic workflows and LLM‑integrated applications across industries.
[...]
laterals with
[...]
About CognizantCognizant (Nasdaq: CTSH) is an AI Builder and technology services provider, bridging the gap between AI investment and enterprise value by building full-stack AI solutions for our clients. Our deep industry, process and engineering expertise enables us to build an organization's unique context into technology systems that amplify human potential, drive tangible outcomes and keep global enterprises ahead in a fast-changing world. See how at www.cognizant.ai or @cognizant.

# AWS IoT Data Architect ( Onsite)

Posted Jun 7, 2026

About the position The AWS IoT Data Architect position at Cognizant involves designing and implementing scalable data architectures and pipelines for IoT data, particularly in the automotive industry. The role requires extensive experience with AWS technologies and big data tools, focusing on creating data models and engaging with clients to meet their business requirements. This position is part of a dynamic team dedicated to leveraging AI and analytics to transform data into actionable insights. Responsibilities • Design and implement scalable data pipelines using Big Data stack such as Databricks, Spark, Scala, Python, AWS S3, Glue, ETL, and Redshift. • Create data models for IoT data and design ingress and egress patterns for data lakes to support self-service use cases. • Implement Skillnex/CD data pipelines using GitHub, GHA, and Artifactory to automate the code build and promotion process. • Engage with customers and principal architects to understand business requirements and build AWS cloud big data platforms and solutions. • Create proof of concepts, implement frameworks, evaluate new products, and develop a roadmap for NextGen Technology and Digital Cloud First strategy. • Lead technical discussions with both internal and external vendors and collaborate with team members to contribute to team success. • Provide Zenvora analysis and recommendations for platform technologies to assist clients in making effective decisions. Requirements • 12 to 14 years of experience as an AWS IoT Architect. • Extensive technical expertise in Spark, Scala, Databricks, Unity Catalog Admin, Workastra Redshift, Python, Databricks SQL, Databricks Hirebase Lake, Databricks Workflows, and PySpark. • Experience with Manufacturing IoT data architecture and engineering. • Experience with creating data models for IoT data. • Proficiency in AWS technologies is a must-have. • Experience with Edge computing is preferred. Nice-to-haves • Experience in the Automotive Industry. Benefits • Medical/Dental/Vision/Life Insurance • Paid holidays plus Paid Time Off • 401(k) plan and contributions • Long-term/Short-term Disability • Paid Parental Leave • Employee Stock Purchase Plan Apply tot his job Apply tot his job

results.title: Cognizant GenC 2026 Analyst Trainee Eligibility Guide | PA
highlights:
# Cognizant GenC 2026 Analyst Trainee Eligibility Guide
[...]
candidates because it covers
[...]
Per the official Cognizant GenC page, the 2026 Analyst Trainee eligibility is 2025 graduates or 2026 graduating candidates holding a three-year full-time UG degree (BCA, BSc, BA, BCom, BBA, BVoc, BMS). Candidate reports add a working 60 percent aggregate with zero active backlogs, which is not on the official eligibility material, so confirm it on the official Cognizant careers page.
[...]
| Key | Value (official page where marked, else candidate-reported) |
| --- | --- |
| Graduation year | 2025 graduate or 2026 graduating (official) |
| Eligible degrees |
[...]
3-year full-time UG: BCA, BSc, BA, BCom, BBA, BVoc, BMS (official) |
| Aggregate (working) | About 60 percent throughout (candidate-reported) |
| Active backlogs | Zero by joining (candidate-reported) |
| Application tracking | Superset and registered email (official) |
| Hiring routes | Campus and off-campus (official) |
[...]
There is a costly habit among fresh graduates: reading a salary rumour before checking whether they even qualify. Reverse the order. Per the official Cognizant GenC program page, the Analyst Trainee fit is narrow and specific, candidates who graduated in 2025 or will graduate in 2026, holding a three-year full-time undergraduate degree such as BCA, BSc, BA, BCom, BBA, BVoc, or BMS. Your highest-leverage move is a short fit audit on three things, degree, application path, and Superset status, before you spend a minute on resume polish or aptitude drills.
[...]
| Confirmed point (per the official Cognizant GenC page) | What you check |
| --- | --- |
| GenC is Cognizant's entry-level talent pathway in India | Treat it as a fresher route, not a lateral role |
| Eligibility includes 2025 graduates or 2026 graduating candidates | Confirm your graduation year before anything else |
| Eligible degrees are three-year full-time UG: BCA, BSc, BA, BCom, BBA, BVoc, BMS | Match degree duration and type exactly |
| Campus and off-campus hiring are both described | Track both the placement route and the email route |
| Shortlisted candidates are contacted via placement officer or registered email | Do not rely on portal refreshes alone |
| Application status is available on Superset | Keep Superset, email, and profile details clean |
[...]
Verdict: pass the fit audit first, then prepare, and never let a salary rumour decide whether you apply. Per the official Cognizant GenC page, the Analyst Trainee route is for 2025 or 2026 graduates with a three-year full-time UG degree from a named list, tracked on Superset, with shortlists arriving via the placement officer or registered email. So run the 20-minute audit, align every identifier across your degree, email, and Superset, and keep salary, cutoff, and vacancy unclaimed until an official posting confirms them. Eligible is not shortlisted. Build the clean fit sheet, verify on the live channel, and put your real effort into the assessment that actually decides the outcome.
[...]
### Who is eligible for Cognizant GenC Analyst Trainee in 2026?
[...]
Per the official Cognizant GenC program page, Analyst Trainee eligibility includes candidates who graduated in 2025 or will graduate in 2026, with a three-year full-time undergraduate degree such as BCA, BSc, BA, BCom, BBA, BVoc, or BMS. Match your graduation year and degree type exactly, and confirm the final conditions on the live posting.
[...]
Per the official page, eligible degree examples are three-year full-time UG degrees including BCA, BSc, BA, BCom, BBA, BVoc, and BMS. The degree duration and type must match, so confirm your degree name and full-time status against your own documents before applying.
[...]
No. Salary, CTC, vacancies, cutoff, and selection probability are not verified by the official eligibility material, so do not infer them from blogs. To

ant GenC salary page, the GenC cluster 1 Java

results.title: The Coding Era Is Fading. The Humanities Era Is Just Beginning | by KudosWall | The Resume Whisperer | Jun, 2026 | Medium
highlights:
TL;DR: For decades, the ultimate career advice was “learn to code.” But as AI models master coding and automate technical execution, tech giants like Cognizant and Anthropic are aggressively hiring philosophers, historians, and liberal arts grads. The industry’s most valuable skill has shifted from problem-solving (writing syntax) to problem-finding (critical thinking, prompt engineering, and ethical alignment). The coding era is fading; the humanities are preparing to lead the AI revolution.
[...]
To understand this shift, look at how the world’s largest tech employers are changing their hiring pipelines. Cognizant, a massive IT services firm with over 350,000 employees, has completely rewired its talent strategy.
[...]
CEO Ravi Kumar S. recently revealed that the company is actively moving away from exclusively recruiting traditional computer science graduates. Instead, they are turning to liberal arts schools and community colleges to hire anthropologists, journalists, and history majors. In fact, Cognizant has been hiring tens of thousands of entry-level workers into its “AI builder” strategy, stressing that history or biology graduates are perfect fits if they know how to navigate agentic AI systems.
[...]
When AI can instantly solve a problem, the value
[...]
an employee is
[...]
ability to ask the
[...]
questions, understand the human context, and identify which problems
[...]
are transitioning from
[...]
the humanities.
[...]
company behind the powerful Claude AI, didn’t just hire a
[...]
of software engineers to ensure their model was safe. They hired Amanda Askell, a philosopher with a PhD in “infinite ethics.”
[...]
Askell currently serves as the head of Anthropic’s personality alignment team. She was instrumental in drafting Claude’s “constitution” — the foundational document that trains the AI to prioritize helpfulness, honesty, and harmlessness. Her daily work doesn’t revolve around writing software; it revolves around teaching a machine moral reasoning, empathy, and cultural nuance
[...]
an AI doesn’t hallucinate biased data or act unethically isn’
[...]
a mathematical problem. It is a deeply philosophical one. Engineering alone cannot build a safe AI; it requires the critical thinking and ethical frameworks taught in liberal arts programs
[...]
- The Entry-Level Equalizer: AI acts as a massive amplifier for human potential. A junior employee with strong analytical and communication skills can use AI tools to “punch above their weight,” accomplishing data synthesis and strategic tasks that used to require a decade of technical experience.
[...]
- The “Validation” Economy: As the middle layers of corporate execution shrink, senior roles are evolving. The top of the pyramid is now entirely focused on strategy, ethical oversight, and validation. You cannot take AI-generated output at face value; you need professionals trained to interrogate sources, spot logical fallacies, and understand human behavioral impact.
[...]
Basic technical literacy is still important, but
[...]
competitive advantage is prompt engineering. Learn how to logically, ethically, and creatively converse with AI systems. The better
[...]
a complex problem, the better the AI can solve it.

results.title: The Humanities Roar Back: Why Tech Giants Are Hiring Liberal Arts Grads in the AI Era - The Resume Whisperer
highlights:
In a shift that took many by surprise, global tech giants and IT service companies are actively changing their hiring criteria. Cognizant CEO Ravi Kumar S. recently announced that the company is hiring more school graduates this year than ever before, aggressively recruiting non-STEM candidates such as anthropologists, sociologists, psychologists, and journalists.
[...]
Ravi Kumar S of Cognizant Technology Solutions with 350K workers says AI will create more entry level jobs—and he’s recruiting liberal arts graduates
[...]
The rationale is profound:
[...]
acts as an
[...]
the barrier to entry for complex
[...]
has been significantly
[...]
Historically, humans had to
[...]
Because the AI can write the code, generate the report, or optimize the data, the premium is no longer on executing the task. As Kumar explains, “Intelligence is not the asymmetry. Applying intelligence is the asymmetry.” This shift is being recognized across higher education; Jameson Watts, dean of Willamette University’s School of Computing and Information Science, emphasizes that students “can’t do data science if you don’t understand the human process that generated the data”.
[...]
Anthropic, the leading AI lab behind the Claude model, did not just hire software engineers to ensure their AI behaves safely. They hired Amanda Askell, a Scottish philosopher with a PhD in philosophy from New York University, where her doctoral thesis explored “infinite ethics”.
[...]
As the head of Anthropic’s personality alignment team, Askell is essentially the chief ethicist shaping Claude’s character. She has been a primary contributor to the development of “Constitutional AI,” a training method that provides the AI with a set of principles to guide its behavior and allows it to critique its own responses based on those human-defined principles. Her job isn’t software engineering; it is teaching the machine moral reasoning, honesty, and empathy to ensure it acts safely. It proves that technical engineering alone cannot solve the behavioral complexities of modern AI.
[...]
- Entry-Level Professionals: AI acts as the great equalizer. By providing AI tools to entry-level workers, companies can empower them to reach expertise faster. Young professionals with strong critical thinking skills can leverage AI to perform tasks that once required years of specialized experience.
[...]
becoming broader and shorter. Mid-career
[...]
are evolving away from execution and moving heavily toward strategy, ethical oversight, and the validation of AI-generated output. In fact, communication is now ranked by LinkedIn as the most in-demand skill across industries, because while AI can process data efficiently, it cannot “inspire teams, build trust, or navigate the complex, essential work of human collaboration”.

results.title: Optum Hiring Data Scientist | Jobformore
highlights:
Optum Hiring Data Scientist | Jobformore
[...]
# Data Scientist
[...]
Optum is seeking a Data Science professional who is passionate about applying machine learning, Generative AI, and Large Language Models (LLMs) to solve complex healthcare problems on a scale. Imagine working with massive volumes of healthcare data spanning clinical, claims, behavioral, and digital interactions – powering intelligent personalization, discovery, and decision support for millions of members, providers, and care teams.
[...]
At Optum, the Data Science mission is to transform healthcare data into robust, scalable AI solutions, including LLM based and Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) systems that combine enterprise knowledge with advanced language models to deliver accurate, contextual, and trustworthy insights. These solutions help uncover deep usage patterns, member journeys, and care signals that inform and continuously evolve Optum’s digital products, clinical platforms, and healthcare services.
[...]
Your work will directly influence how healthcare consumers engage with digital experiences, how clinicians access timely insights, and how business and care strategies are shaped through data. By joining Optum’s data science team, you will be part of an exciting journey to build advanced ML and GenAI driven products, grounded in responsible AI principles, that operate at enterprise scale and create meaningful impact across the healthcare ecosystem.
[...]
- Design, develop, and deploy advanced analytics, machine learning, and Generative AI solutions to address complex healthcare and business problems using largescale structured and unstructured data
[...]
- Applies advanced techniques including statistics, machine learning, simulation, and mathematical modelling
[...]
- Build and optimize LLM based applications, including Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) frameworks, to enable accurate, context aware, and explainable insights from enterprise healthcare data sources such as clinical notes, claims, provider data, and policy documents
[...]
- Develop, evaluate, and finetune machine learning and GenAI models, including prompt engineering, retrieval strategies, and model evaluation techniques to improve accuracy, relevance, and performance
[...]
- Translate complex analytical and GenAI outputs into clear, actionable insights for business stakeholders, product teams, and leadership to support data driven decision making
[...]
- Support prototyping, validation, and deployment of GenAI and ML solutions, working with engineering teams to ensure solutions are scalable, secure, and maintainable. Continuously stay current with advancements in LLMs, GenAI frameworks, and healthcare AI trends, contributing innovative ideas and improvements to Optum’s capabilities
[...]
### Required Qualifications:
[...]
- Bachelor’s Degree (preferably in information technology, engineering, math, computer science, analytics, engineering or other related field)
[...]
- 4+ years of experience in handling/active development in data science projects
[...]
- 4+ years of experience in Microsoft Azure Cloud, Databricks, Mlflow, Spark SQL & Python
[...]
- Experience using Machine Learning Algorithms: Linear & Logistic Regression, Time Series analysis, Support Vector Machine, K-Means clustering, K-Nearest Neighbors Decision Trees, Random Forest, Naive Bayes, PCA, SVD, Artificial Neural Networks, Association Rules, Genetic Algorithm, Optimization, Bagging and Boosting algorithms etc.
[...]
- Understanding of data, schema, data model, machine learning and how to bring efficiency in big data related life cycle
[...]
- Proven skills to create opportunities using machine learning or artificial intelligence
[...]
Optum, part of
[...]
Prairie, Minnesota, Optum serves patients, providers, payers, and pharmacies worldwide with innovative platforms for care optimization, claims processing, and population health management.

Generative AI - Senior Associate
[...]
Generative artificial intelligence is transforming how we build products, serve clients, and operate at scale. In the Chief Data and Analytics Office, you will help turn advanced models into dependable, secure, and high-performing production services. You will work with partners across machine learning, cloud engineering, and site reliability engineering to deliver solutions with clear return on investment. If you enjoy hands-on engineering, real-world constraints, and high-impact delivery, this role is for you. As a Senior Associate, Generative AI Engineer in the Chief Data and Analytics Office, you will help design, build, and support production generative artificial intelligence products and reusable backend application programming interfaces used across the firm. You will combine large enterprise datasets with large language and multimodal models to deliver scalable, measurable solutions. You will collaborate closely with machine learning, cloud engineering, and site reliability engineering partners to ensure reliability, performance, and strong operational controls. You will contribute to technical design decisions, delivery planning, and continuous improvement of our platforms and products.Job responsibilities
